heading: Effect on other law
label: 6511 Effect on other law
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)651141:42., .June 30, 1936, ch. 881, § 849 Stat. 2039Paragraph (1) is substituted for “Title III of the act entitled ‘An Act making appropriations for the Treasury and Post Office Departments for the fiscal year ending , and for other purposes’, approved (commonly known as the Buy American Act)” for consistency in the revised title and to correct an error in the source, which incorrectly gives , rather than , as the date of approval.June 30, 1934May 3, 1933May 3, 1933March 3, 1933Paragraph (2) is substituted for “the Act entitled ‘An Act relating to the rate of wages for laborers and mechanics employed on public buildings of the United States and the District of Columbia by contractors and subcontractors, and for other purposes’, approved (commonly known as the Bacon-Davis Act), as amended from time to time” because of (40 U.S.C. note prec. 101) and for consistency with title 40.March 3, 1931section 5(c) of Public Law 107–217The words “the labor provisions of Title II of the National Industrial Recovery Act, approved , as extended” are omitted as obsolete because of (ch. 554, ), as amended by the Acts of (ch. 437, ), (ch. 40, ), and (ch. 450, ).June 16, 1933June 27, 1940April 5, 1941June 27, 1942section 201 of the Act of June 21, 193852 Stat. 81654 Stat. 63355 Stat. 11056 Stat. 410The words “or [the labor provisions] of section 7 of the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act, approved ” are omitted as obsolete. The intended reference was probably to section 7 of the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1935 (). Section 7 of the Emergency Relief Appropriation Act of 1935 provided that the President shall require certain rates of pay for persons engaged in carrying out projects that were financed by amounts being appropriated in that Act.April 8, 193549 Stat. 118Paragraph (3) is substituted for “the Act entitled ‘An Act to provide for the diversification of employment of Federal prisoners, for their training and schooling in trades and occupations, and for other purposes’, approved , as amended and supplemented by the Act approved ” for consistency with title 18. The Act of (ch. 340, ) and the Act of (ch. 736, ), which were classified to sections 744a to 744n of former title 18, were substantially repealed and were replaced by chapter 307 and in the codification of title 18 by the Act of (ch. 645, ). Subsequently, was repealed by ().May 27, 1930June 23, 1934May 27, 1930June 23, 1934June 25, 194846 Stat. 39148 Stat. 1211section 4162 of title 1862 Stat. 683section 4162 of title 18section 218(a)(4) of Public Law 98–47398 Stat. 2027
